fields.description:
## Why

Epic `850f`'s seam (S2) is inert until a real backend plugs in. This story adds the v1 T2 backend —
one-shot `pyright --outputjson` diagnostics for Python — behind S2's `refute_semantic` dispatch,
turning the abstain-by-default seam into a working confirm-only semantic resolver. It is self-contained
and opt-in: gated behind an optional extra plus `t2_enabled`, and it abstains (never raises) when
pyright is absent.

## What

- New module `src/rebar/grounding/pyright_backend.py`:
  - A `refute(reference, *, repo_root, timeout, cache=None) -> dict` that `semantic.refute_semantic`
    dispatches to when `t2_backend == "pyright"`. Runs `pyright --outputjson` over the **project root**
    (not single-file, so cross-module imports resolve) via the fail-open `harness.run_tool` with the
    configured timeout; caches the parsed result in the caller-owned `cache` keyed by project root
    (one invocation per review, reused across references).
  - **Confirm-only mapping** for a reference in file `in_file`:
    - `refuted@T2` iff pyright ran and produced parseable JSON, AND `in_file` has **zero**
      import-resolution diagnostics (`reportMissingImports` / `reportMissingModuleSource` = env built),
      AND no diagnostic in `in_file` "concerns the reference" (an unresolved-kind rule —
      `reportAttributeAccessIssue` / `reportUndefinedVariable` / unresolved-import — whose message
      names the reference's leaf `name`). Since `validate_reference` carries no line/span, matching is
      by file + rule-kind + the leaf name appearing in the diagnostic message.
    - `abstain@T2` (closed reason) otherwise: `no_tool` (pyright absent), `unsupported_lang`
      (reference language is not Python), `ambiguous` (no `in_file` to locate), `parse_error`
      (unparseable output), `timeout`, `other` (crash / env-not-built / a diagnostic sits at the
      reference — a suspected-absent is never asserted). Any diagnostic rule the mapping does not
      recognize routes to `abstain`, never `refuted` (fail-safe).
  - A `version() -> str | None` PATH/`--version` probe (fail-open) and registration into
    `semantic.T2_BACKENDS` + `available_backends()`.
- Add the optional extra `grounding-t2 = ["pyright>=1.1"]` to `pyproject.toml`; guard the pyright
  invocation so a non-adopting client (extra not installed / pyright not on PATH) pays nothing and gets
  `abstain(no_tool)`.
- Docs: a T2 section in `docs/grounding.md` describing the backend, the confirm-only mapping, the three
  `.rebar/grounding.toml` keys, and the `grounding-t2` extra.
- Tests: `tests/unit/grounding/test_pyright_backend.py` — a fail-open matrix pinned against captured
  real `pyright --outputjson` fixtures.

## Scope

In scope: `pyright_backend.py`, the `grounding-t2` extra, `docs/grounding.md` T2 section, fixtures +
tests, and registering the backend into S2's `T2_BACKENDS`. Depends on S2's seam. Out of scope:
additional languages/backends (future work behind the same seam).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/grounding/pyright_backend.py` implements `refute`, `version`, and registers `"pyright"`
      into `semantic.T2_BACKENDS` and `available_backends()`.
- [ ] With `t2_enabled=true, t2_backend="pyright"`, a member/dotted reference that resolves in a Python
      fixture whose imports all resolve yields `refuted` at `TIER_T2`.
- [ ] The same reference with an unresolved attribute (imports otherwise resolving) yields `abstain` at
      `TIER_T2` (never an asserted absence).
- [ ] Fail-open matrix, each mapping to `abstain` with the stated closed reason: pyright off `PATH`
      → `no_tool`; non-Python reference → `unsupported_lang`; reference with no `in_file` → `ambiguous`;
      unparseable pyright output → `parse_error`; simulated timeout → `timeout`; a file with
      `reportMissingImports` (env-not-built) → `other`; a subprocess crash → `other`.
- [ ] A test asserts `refute` returns only `refuted` or `abstain` outcomes across the whole matrix
      (the confirm-only guarantee).
- [ ] `pyproject.toml` declares the `grounding-t2` extra.
- [ ] A test with pyright removed from `PATH` shows the backend returns `abstain(no_tool)` without
      raising.
- [ ] `grounding_info()` lists the pyright backend with its detected availability/version.
- [ ] `grounding_info()` output validates against `grounding_info.schema.json` after the change.
- [ ] `docs/grounding.md` has a T2 section documenting the backend, the mapping, the config keys, and
      the extra.
- [ ] `pytest tests/unit/grounding/` exits 0.
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0.
